title: Spiritual implications of metaphysical biology
wikitext:
[]: Spiritual implications of metaphysical biology             Quote Post by Lavinius » Fri Oct 23, 2015 6:59 pm I mean like just think about it. What reason do we have to think he's not?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Oct 23, 2015 8:05 pm What reason do we have to think he is? And why should we care either way?

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Fri Oct 23, 2015 8:28 pm Gendou clearly follows our lord and savior, the Colonel Sanders. He fried for our sins.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Fri Oct 23, 2015 8:30 pm Lavinus is secretly a witch, and we need to burn him at the stake. I mean, what reason do we have to believe that he isn't a witch?

[]:             Quote Post by IronEvangelion » Fri Oct 23, 2015 8:49 pm If Gendo was a member of any Abrahamic religion, I don't think he'd be in the line of work he's in (slaying the messengers of his own god). Unless he was from an extremely liberal sect. So there's some circumstantial evidence against him being a Muslim, but it can't be disproven outright as his religious beliefs are never stated in the show. Personally I think he only worships 2 people: Yui, and his own reflection in the mirror.

[]:             Quote Post by cyharding » Fri Oct 23, 2015 9:14 pm I always assumed he was an atheist. I keep thinking of the "Science has protected us" line from when he and Fuyutsuki were in Antarctica retrieving the spear.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Fri Oct 23, 2015 9:29 pm I'm in line with the atheist point of view. I'd be inclined to believe that everybody working at NERV is-- they have clear evidence of extraterrestrial life, and enough weird shit happens on a daily basis that I think it would be enough to shake anybody's faith. As to your idea, Lavinius, the null hypothesis states that Gendo isn't religious. Provide a reason why he is if you want to overturn the null hypothesis.

[]:             Quote Post by El Squibbonator » Fri Oct 23, 2015 9:35 pm Also, keep in mind that this is Japan we're talking about. Japan has a very small population of followers of Abrahamic religions--the majority are either Buddhist (which curiously never seems to be mentioned in EVA) or are "unaffiliated."

[]:             Quote Post by mehdi_saada » Sat Oct 24, 2015 5:09 am More than atheist, Gendou and all NERV are over-positivist : science is better than nature, than God, science will grant us salvation. Well, they better think that if they want to live. Nothing surprising, as NERV is the embodiment of what's left of Science in the post 2I world. Fuyutsuki seems wiser.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sat Oct 24, 2015 5:20 am mehdi_saada wrote: More than atheist, Gendou and all NERV are over-positivist : science is better than nature, than God, science will grant us salvation. Well, they better think that if they want to live. Nothing surprising, as NERV is the embodiment of what's left of Science in the post 2I world. Fuyutsuki seems wiser.

[]:             Quote Post by mehdi_saada » Sat Oct 24, 2015 5:38 am I think it was made clear, NERV concentrates most of the ressources left in the world after 2I, and the most advanced scientifical advances made so far by humanity. That's what is said by Gendou to Fuyu in the flash back episode 21(DC ? dunno). And NERV is also heavily funded by ONU, whereas the others(Jet-Alone for instance) or not(I remember it is the Japanese army ?). So whereas the other spots of heavy science in Eva-world are necessary for the storyline, I still think NERV represents science in our world, and the way it deals with thoses things around sexuality and the mass-nevrosis problem. That makes of NERV the embodiment of psychanalysis, psychiatry, the shrinks. Who the hell does one go to see for inner troubles ? A priest ? Not so much nowadays.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sat Oct 24, 2015 5:46 am mehdi_saada wrote: That makes of NERV the embodiment of psychanalysis, psychiatry, the shrinks. Who the hell does one go to see for inner troubles ? A priest ? Not so much nowadays.

[]:             Quote Post by Moap » Sun Oct 25, 2015 1:15 am I think the chances of a Japanese man being a muslim are very low

[]:             Quote Post by mehdi_saada » Sun Oct 25, 2015 5:01 am Once I thought about it more deeply, I think the best characterization is satanist. Because : all he says, seem to indicate he believes in god, or in godly entities (and he messes with two of them on a regular basis) but, he wants merely to use their power, to overtake them. Is that not what a satanist do ? To believe, yet do evil things because you don't care or you carry a grudge against the fucking creator that took away your wife from you ? Because you think god is wrong ? It's a bit too much, but it brings about the interesting idea : scientist + belief in god = satanist I thought so from the start, that the EvaGod or the will of the FAR whatever, wanted our current relation to life(system) to change drasticaly, then are brought about Angels, whose names are more or less each and every one related to a part of the problem. But we are so much identified to the said system, that their will equals to us to killing us. Like in the Matrix : you switch off the Matrix, you kill the people. You need a thorough inner work to discover there's an other, more genuine and true conception of life, and that's what 3I brought about. Godly advices that our way of life is wrong (acts of angels) are not enough because we perceive pain in our life mostly as an foe, not an advice. We want oblivion and escape from what's painful, not understanding. That's Shinji's attitude all along the show. Please don't throw at my face that Angels actually want to kill us, imagine that the show is a symbol for what happens in the real world. A death in the Eva-world, would convey the idea of a spiritual death, or symbolic death of what the character stands for, rather than an actual but meaningless death only for storyline needs.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Oct 25, 2015 5:51 am We're talking Japan here, where Shinto's pantheistic nature would inform idiom in a different fashion to the Sky Father assumptions at the other end of the Eurasian land mass. And in the setting, the advances in metabiology mean that Applied Theology is just an engineering discipline, rather than being a specialization within diplomacy.

[]: Original Asia. BP             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Sun Oct 25, 2015 5:58 am Nuclear Lunchbox wrote: I'm in line with the atheist point of view. I'd be inclined to believe that everybody working at NERV is-- they have clear evidence of extraterrestrial life, and enough weird shit happens on a daily basis that I think it would be enough to shake anybody's faith.

[]:             Quote Post by xPearse » Sun Oct 25, 2015 7:27 am You people are reading far too much into things.

[]:             Quote Post by unz » Sun Oct 25, 2015 8:28 am I lack the motivation to look for graveyards in any evangelion thing and if he was muslim or anything they would have told us, to me he's mostly a big nerd if that counts. Following a death cult. "Old men are the future"

[]:             Quote Post by mehdi_saada » Sun Oct 25, 2015 11:12 am what , are you still talking about Gendou ? He said " death won't create anything"(or about the same) in EoE, so he ain't a death cult's follower, leave that to the creepy old farts. I said satanists, not death seeker. Personaly, he may just be be amoral and doesn't care, and live for his own desires. Very representative of most humans' way of life. Yet, he does not seek death . I haven't seen before, but pantheist beliefs make the whole situation less unbearable for believers. I mean, if gods litteraly are filling up the air, being in conflict with some of them is less portentous than fighting the ONE AND ONLY god !

[]:             Quote Post by unz » Sun Oct 25, 2015 11:45 am I don't really feel like talking about gendo but this death cult finds meaning in the afterlife through instrumentality, which gendo needs for pussy, so you have that but still nerdy deploying german language because Idk everything sounds too funny in Japanese to be taken seriously from strangers.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Oct 23, 2015 8:05 pm What reason do we have to think he is? And why should we care either way?
